heading: Cases involving DNA evidence
label: 3297 Cases involving DNA evidence
text: In a case in which DNA testing implicates an identified person in the commission of a felony, no statute of limitations that would otherwise preclude prosecution of the offense shall preclude such prosecution until a period of time following the implication of the person by DNA testing has elapsed that is equal to the otherwise applicable limitation period.
notes: Amendments2006— struck out “except for a felony offense under chapter 109A,” before “no statute of limitations”.Pub. L. 109–162

Effective Date, , , provided that:Pub. L. 108–405, title II, § 204(c)118 Stat. 2271Oct. 30, 2004“The amendments made by this section [enacting this section] shall apply to the prosecution of any offense committed before, on, or after the date of the enactment of this section [] if the applicable limitation period has not yet expired.”Oct. 30, 2004
